sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.28 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a connected linear reductive algebraic group over a field of positive characteristic,$X$a closed subset of~$G$, and let$X^k=\{x_1 x_k x_i X\}$. a) Is it true that there always exists a positive integer$c=c(X)>1$such that$X^c$is closed? b) If$X$is a conjugacy class of$G$such that$X^2$contains an open subset of$G$, then is$X^2=G$? { E.\,P.\,Vdovin}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
